turns.user_message: I tried to distract myself by organizing my whole space today, but it just made me feel worse instead of better. I kept staring at the mess and wondering why I can never just keep things together like other people seem to. It feels like I put in all this effort for absolutely nothing, and the clutter just piles right back up anyway. I guess I should have known it would end up like this, since I always mess up the simple stuff.

I have been trying to distract myself with new routines for months to feel better, but the same heavy feeling just follows me everywhere. Every little project I start turns into proof that I am not capable of handling basic stuff. I watch how easily everyone else moves through their days, and it just highlights how stuck I really am. It is exhausting to pretend I have it together when I know I am just fumbling through everything.

I honestly do not know why I keep forcing myself to try these little fixes when the outcome is always exactly the same. I pour so much energy into cheering myself up, only to end up feeling completely worthless when it all falls apart. It is like I am fundamentally broken and nothing I do will ever actually change that. I just sit here watching everything I touch slip through my fingers, knowing I will never be enough.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This has been going on for a while.
